reasoning:
To resolve YES, Andrew Cuomo must both win the June 24, 2025 NYC Dem Mayoral Primary AND do so in 5 rounds or fewer. Polling (as of mid-June) shows Cuomo leading with 31-35% in the first round. However, the field is extremely fragmented, with 9 recognized candidates participating in debates. In a Ranked-Choice Voting (RCV) system, reaching the 50% threshold from a ~35% start requires the elimination of multiple mid-to-lower-tier candidates, each of which accounts for a separate tabulation round unless batch-eliminated (which is mathematically difficult for mid-tier candidates). 

A recent Marist poll explicitly simulated the RCV tabulation for this specific field and calculated that Cuomo would not cross the 50% threshold until the 7th round. Furthermore, there is also a non-trivial chance that Cuomo loses the primary altogether to Zohran Mamdani, whose support has surged to ~27%, which would also resolve the question to NO. The combination of his ~15-19 point deficit from 50%, the size of the field, the mechanics of NYC RCV counting, and his risk of simply losing makes it highly improbable he wins in 5 rounds or fewer.
